architecture_fit.note: Single base_model.py attention head, fixed φ=[x,y,x²+y²,1] + hand-set 4×4 W_q/W_k so Q·K=-‖c_i-c_j‖²; minimal attention-only delta, no MLP, perfect NN argmax.

baseline_comparison.note: Random policy (0.52→0.11) plus two ablation strawmen measured under identical conditions; method (1.0) clearly dominates.

faithfulness.note: Ablating the necessary key-norm -s_j feature collapses accuracy toward random (0.76→0.54), while the inert query-norm s_i ablation leaves it at 1.0 — pinning the load-bearing feature.

operating_range.note: nn_accuracy=1.0 and size_robustness=1.0 across N=5..40; score equals true distance analytically so it generalizes beyond the tested span.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-set frozen torch weights, no training, reproducing the target exactly — strongest evidence of understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo shows score-vs-(-dist²) collapsing onto y=x as direct proof, argmax-edge scatter, and a grouped ablation/baseline bar chart — claim is legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties the y=x scatter to the negative-distance proof and the bar chart's y-axis/baselines/ablations to which feature is load-bearing.

notes: A textbook hand-built circuit: it shows that an ordinary dot-product attention head computes negative squared distance once the key carries a -‖k‖² feature, so the NN heuristic falls straight out of attention. Perfect benchmark scores (nn_accuracy=1.0, size_robustness=1.0) are backed by a genuine causal ablation that isolates the one load-bearing feature and a clear two-view visualisation. Minimal delta from base_model.py, fully frozen weights, and an inert-feature control make every rubric dimension land.
